workflows-reference.purpose: Provide workflows for preserving and extending an existing project architecture.

documentation-context.rule: Inspect existing code, docs, tests, package scripts, and CI before choosing an implementation shape.
When docs are still draft, fill them from observed evidence before broad implementation work.

workflow.description: Add one capability by observing, preserving, and extending the existing architecture.

enforcement.rule: Run phases sequentially: evidence discovery, fit design, implementation.
Do not introduce competing architecture names or folders without a decision record.
Implementation must use the existing composition, testing, naming, and dependency style unless a change is approved.

phase.name: evidence-discovery
phase.purpose: Discover how the current project already organizes similar behavior.

actions.action: Read existing lock docs and inspect similar modules, tests, commands, and composition points.
Summarize the current architecture, principles, workflow, and commands from evidence.
Identify the smallest existing place where the new behavior belongs.

phase-output.existing-evidence: Files, docs, commands, and conventions observed.
phase-output.placement: Likely module, package, or component to extend.

phase.name: fit-design
phase.purpose: Design the feature to fit the current architecture instead of replacing it.

actions.action: Define behavior and tests in the project's existing style.
State how the design follows existing boundaries and naming.
List any architecture tension and whether it requires a decision record.
Define verification using existing scripts or commands.

phase-output.docs-impact: Existing lock docs to fill or update.

phase.name: implementation
phase.purpose: Implement one approved slice while preserving existing structure.

actions.action: Do not create new root architecture folders without approval.

phase-output.architecture-fit: How the change fits existing structure.

workflow.evidence: Observed architecture evidence, approved fit design, existing-style tests, and updated lock docs when needed.

steps.step: Find the existing owner and similar behavior.
Add or update tests in the existing style.
Change the smallest existing module or component.
State why existing architecture can no longer absorb the change.
Compare preserving, adapting, and replacing the existing pattern.
Implement migration in small compatibility-preserving slices.
Verify existing behavior and new boundary behavior.

workflow.evidence: Decision record, compatibility evidence, and verification.
